[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / take] Can an unprivileged maker, after a taker has partially filled an offer (consumed[maker][group] > 0), call setIsRootRatifier(maker, root, false) to revoke the Merkle root mid-sequence, under the precondition that the offer's group still has remaining capacity and the taker intends to fill again in a subsequent transaction, triggering RatifierFail() on the second take call, violating the invariant that a partially-consumed offer with remaining capacity must remain fillable until explicitly cancelled or expired, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of the taker's ability to complete the fill and locking their partial position? [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker supply a leafIndex where leafIndex >> proof.length != 0, under the precondition that the ratifierData is crafted with a large leafIndex and a short proof array, causing HashLib.isLeaf to revert with LeafIndexOutOfRange(), violating the invariant that invalid ratifier data must revert cleanly without corrupting state, causing scoped impact: griefing of the take call with a revert that wastes gas but leaves consumed state unchanged?
